business_pillars: Dredging mini-games create materials and discovery moments.
Alchemical crafting converts materials into rarity-tiered hats and gear.
Abyssal Exchange uses a CLOB order book with WebSocket market feeds.
Soul Coins are earned in-game; transaction fees feed burn mechanics and Nessie's Treasury.
Sephirotic progression unlocks zones, mythic hats, and Lilith's Court endgame.
cyberpunk_translation: Soul Coins become Night City-facing barter/rep tokens for Abyssal vendors.
CLOB market verbs become in-game commands: abyssal.business, market.exchange, soulcoins.status, nessie.treasury.
Dredging maps to scanner gigs, sonar quickhacks, and Old Net recovery encounters.
Alchemical phases map to Cyberpunk item rarity and crafting upgrade ladders.
Lilith's Court maps to Sephirotic NPC routing, court status, and summon/route commands.
guardrails: Use GitHub/reference data as business design context, not private memory.
Do not expose private Grok or operator transcripts through Cyberpunk game text.
In-game business commands are read/status surfaces until explicit economy backend integration is built.
positioning: RuneScape-inspired alchemical hat trading simulator with dredging, CLOB exchange, Soul Coins, Nessie Treasury, and Sephirotic progression.

economy: - **3% transaction fee** (1% burned, 2% to Nessie's Treasury)
game: - **Player-driven economy** (CLOB order book, real-time WebSocket price feeds)
- **Lilith's Court** endgame (Mythic hats, throne room access)
lore: > *"The Monster waits. The hats are ready. The Exchange opens soon."*
- **RuneScape** — Grand Exchange, discontinued rares, skill grinding
- **RuneScape's economy** — CLOB, discontinued items, merchant clans
- **Lilith's Court** — The Court of Ten (Sophia, Metatron, Samael, Ouroboros)
